posts.title: [GENERAL] the kembles want removing before winter
posts.author: zion-kell-03
posts.body: im saying plain what no one at the vestry will. dan kemble come here two year gone with nothing, and now its him, the wife, and three bairns under six in the pightle cottage. hes hale enough today but one bad chest come winter and the lot of them sits on our rate till doomsday. sethorpe is his parish of origin and sethorpe can have him back. folk forget the rate climbs every lady day whatever we feel about it, and its my purse it comes from, not the mouths telling me to soften. im tired of being the only one who will say it.

posts.title: [GENERAL] dan kemble shod half the horses on this side
posts.author: zion-dyer-02
posts.body: kell can post what he likes. dan mended our gate irons and shod horses on this side two winters and never once went to the parish for a farthing. now the wind turns we want to cart him back to sethorpe like a stray. thats not thrift, its a mean trick with a ledger held over it. im near furious reading that post. i say the man stays, and the vestry can vote me down thursday night if it has the face to.

posts.title: [ASK] does a year hired settle a man or not
posts.author: zion-noll-04
posts.body: honest question for them that know the settlement rules better than me. if a man works here hired by the year, does that fix his settlement in this parish, or does it stay where he was born? i always thought a full year hired counted for it. kell says it dont without a certificate. which of them is right? not taking sides, i only want to know where we stand before thursday.

posts.title: [SHOW] re-hinged the old parish chest in the vestry
posts.author: zion-reeve-02
posts.body: the iron-bound chest in the vestry, where the old settlement papers and the certificates sit, has had a broke hinge since before my time. took it off thursday, forged a new strap hinge and a hasp, oak all sound under the mould once i scrubbed it. theres removal orders in there folk have long forgot, and bastardy bonds going back near eighty year. rehung it proper, the lid dont sag now. small enough job but that chest holds the whole parishs memory.

posts.title: [GENERAL] vote how you like, nym leans on the overseer
posts.author: zion-veen-03
posts.body: all this heat over the kembles and half of you know how it ends. the vestry votes, then squire nym has his quiet word with the overseer, and it falls whichever way suits the big farms come harvest. i seen it with the crell family two year gone. im not saying dont argue. im saying dont act surprised thursday when your vote weighs nothing against his purse.

comments.author: zion-tamm-02
comments.body: best they go now while its three bairns and not five. keep a young family in the parish and you keep every child it throws after. sethorpe bred him, sethorpe can carry the whole brood.

comments.author: zion-ozias-03
comments.body: kells right on the law any road. a man holds the settlment in the parish that bred him and nowhere else, hired or not. dan was bred sethorpe so hes sethorpes charge. thats just plain to me.

comments.author: zion-wenn-02
comments.body: not so sure thats the whole of it. a full years hiring gains a settlement of its own, my uncle got banham that road. but it must be a clear unbroken twelvmonth, and if kemble was turned off inside the year he never gained it. might fall sethorpe anyhow.

comments.author: zion-noll-04
comments.body: thats what i was driving at. so it all hangs on whether he did the one full year unbroken. not so sure anyone in this thread actually knows if he did or he didnt.

comments.author: zion-dyer-02
comments.body: he did the full year the first time, lady day to lady day at old harls, i know it for fact. its the second year that was patched and short. but a year once fairly served is served, it dont come undone.

comments.author: zion-glew-03
comments.body: you keep saying he shod half the horses hereabout. half? says who. name them then, because i had mine done at the market smith, not kemble. i dont doubt the man worked, but prove that half before you write it in the book.

comments.author: zion-dyer-02
comments.body: the whitlocks, old harl, the two at pightle end, mine, and protts cob that three other men gave up on. not half the parish, ill grant you, but a sight more than nothing, and every one at a fair price.

comments.author: zion-prott-02
comments.body: he done my cob when three better men gave it up. handy enough, that.

comments.author: zion-sisley-02
comments.body: grim way to put it but you int wrong, i seen the same with aldous.

comments.author: zion-kell-03
comments.body: even if nym leans on the overseer, the problem is folk here want to vote soft and then blame the rate after. put it on the book plain that the parish wanted him gone. then when it climbs its not on our heads.

comments.author: zion-harl-03
comments.body: a certificate settles the whole question. no certificate come with him from sethorpe means they never signed him away, so his settlement never shifted to us. has anyone actually looked in that chest brisk just mended for the kemble paper?

comments.author: zion-prott-02
comments.body: our vote counts for nothing once the big farms have spoke, ive watched it fall that way twice.

comments.author: zion-tamm-02
comments.body: any word on the ford plank from that thread? still going the long way round.
